title: Old TAMU Job Dashboard
author: Karina Torres
description:
An automated tracking dashboard for the **TAMU Natural Resources Job Board**. This is the original, gritty vibe-coded prototype built to run seamlessly via a **local backend** before the project transitioned to the cloud!

### ✨ Core Features & Vibe
* **Automated Tracking:** Keeps a steady pulse on ecology job openings.
* **Pure Vibe Energy:** A scrappy, local setup built fast via AI collaboration.

*Note: The upgraded, web-based version is now live on my website!*

title: Ecology Vibe Coding
author: Karina Torres
description:
A goofy little repo blending ecology and AI with pure vibe-coding energy.

### 🌟 Core Features
* **iNat Suite**: Web tools to query taxa, annotate traits, and use CLIP models to explore hidden secondary data.
* **TAMU Job Board**: A serverless dashboard tracking ecology jobs, automated via GitHub Actions.
* **Local Scripts**: Quick image cropping and custom GBIF mapping.

title: Kelp Forest Simulator
author: MarineCoder
description:
An interactive canvas simulation showing **kelp growth rates** based on nutrient values, ambient temperature, and sea urchin populations. Built entirely using vibe coding in `p5.js`!

### Key Features:
- Real-time parameter controls
- Dynamic ecosystem balancing
- Beautiful organic movements

title: Bioluminescent UI
author: GlowWorm
description:
A dark-mode dashboard tailored for *deep-sea exploration* data visualization. Features custom glow-effects in CSS and simulated real-time telemetry inputs.

Created during the Deep Ocean vibe party in Jan.

title: Canopy Tracker
author: TreeHugger99
description: A geospatial visualizer mapping forest canopy height using public satellite datasets. Helps track **deforestation** and canopy recovery over time. Uses `Leaflet.js` for mapping.
